justification: Both domain rules present with testable_assertions that are specific and actionable. All three architecture decisions present (PATCH endpoint, OrderStateMachine, RefundService) with detailed implementation_notes. acceptance_criteria preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim in the first criterion. terminology_alignment is present and addresses Bird/MJ language differences (invariant, order state, revert-to-pending). task_context is one specific, actionable paragraph. No invented information — all details trace back to the described source summaries. The only minor gap is that the terminology_alignment doesn't explicitly call out that Bird says 'SHIPPED' and MJ says 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state' refer to the same concept of order state vs order status, but the 'order state' entry does reconcile the domain and technical terms adequately.

observations.text: Three architecture decisions instead of the minimum two — includes OrderStateMachine as its own decision with whitelist-over-blacklist guidance, which is faithful to MJ's design intent
ORDER_STATE_INVALID error code preserved verbatim in acceptance_criteria
testable_assertions go beyond restating the rule — they specify behavior for edge cases (e.g., other forbidden regressions like DELIVERED → PENDING)
open_questions are well-scoped and flag genuine gaps (complete state enum, transition matrix, RefundService boundary) without inventing answers
Notes for Shaq provide pragmatic defaults for unresolved questions, preventing implementation blocks
No invented information — contradictions_resolved is explicitly empty rather than fabricated

terminology_alignment doesn't explicitly reconcile Bird's 'order status' language with MJ's 'state' field — the entry covers the concept but doesn't call out the specific terminology mismatch between the two agents

Second acceptance criterion (refund rejection) does not preserve a specific error code the way the first criterion preserves ORDER_STATE_INVALID — though this may reflect the source material not specifying one

    You are implementing the Order Fulfillment state management feature. This includes

    valid transition rules, and a RefundService that gates refund issuance on verified
    order state. Two hard invariants must hold: orders cannot regress from SHIPPED to
    PENDING, and refunds can only be issued against DELIVERED orders. These are not
    soft
